channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI enables paralyzed man to control robotic arm with brain signals
item.description: The combination of artificial intelligence and neuroscience allows a paralyzed man to manipulate a robotic arm by using his brain to imagine movements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions of solar power systems could be at risk of cyber attacks after researchers find flurry of vulnerabilities
item.description: Hackers could exploit weak security in solar inverters, manipulating energy production, stealing user data, and even disrupting entire power networks with alarming ease.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to sync iPhone and iPad – iCloud, Photos, Calendars, and more
item.description: One of the big perks of buying an Apple product is the iCloud ecosystem, which keeps everything updated and connected across your devices – here’s how to sync your iPhone and iPad.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Disney+ in April 2025: Andor season 2, Doctor Who season 15, Dying for Sex, and more
item.description: From the return of hugely popular shows to new movies, here's what you'll want to watch on Disney+ in April.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI, Google AI data centers are under stress after new genAI model launches
item.description: New generative AI (genAI) models introduced this week by Google and OpenAI have put the companies’ data centers under stress — and both companies are trying to catch up to demand.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple, Meta face modest fines as EU seeks to avoid US spat
item.description: Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. are set to be hit by fines under the European Union's Big Tech competition rulebook toward the end of next week, with the iPhone maker facing the added threat of recurring penalties under the bloc's Digital Markets Act.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Hey listen! The live-action Legend of Zelda movie just got a 2027 release date confirmed by Sony and Nintendo
item.description: Sony and Nintendo set March 26, 2027 release date for the live-action film adaptation of The Legend of Zelda video game.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: These 5 new AI tools can help you do everything from managing tasks to improving your public speaking
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Hundreds of AI tools emerge every week. I’ve picked five new ones worth exploring.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department announces approval of armed drones sale to Qatar for nearly $2 billion
item.description: The United States has given an initial approval for the energy-rich Mideast nation of Qatar to buy eight armed MQ-9B Predator drones for its military, an estimated purchase worth nearly $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: As big tech circles, UK government struggles to reap promised AI benefits
item.description: The UK government’s grand plan for AI in the public sector is struggling in the face of growing technological challenges, a report by the Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee (PAC), a bipartisan group of elected members of parliament, has found.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kyndryl extends Google Cloud partnership for AI-based mainframe modernization
item.description: Kyndryl and Google Cloud are expanding their partnership to help customers use generative AI to move data off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Preserve loved ones' voicemails on iPhone
item.description: Saving the voices of loved ones can be a comforting way to keep alive memories. Kurt "the CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to preserve voicemail messages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Security Blunder of the Century: How Signal Was Used for Government Secrets
item.description: A shocking incident involving Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and a journalist has exposed serious security vulnerabilities within the US government.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intro to Alpine.js: A JavaScript framework for minimalists
item.description: I recently backpacked through Big Sur, and after a few days, the inevitable happened: I looked at everything I carried and demanded it justify its presence in my backpack. Making tech choices during the software development process is similar.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle releases ML-optimized GraalVM for JDK 24
item.description: Oracle has released GraalVM for JDK 24, an alternative Java Development Kit tuned to just-released JDK 24 that uses ML (machine learning)-based profile inference to boost peak performance by about 7.9% on average on microservices benchmarks, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise targets IoT, industrial networks with private 5G package
item.description: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise (ALE) has partnered with Celona to offer a turnkey private 5G package for industrial or IoT networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As federal employees return to office, agencies need a reliable, secure system to meet mandates and deliver efficient, productive facilities
item.description: Federal agencies need a unified platform that can deliver real-time reporting on occupancy, space availability, and opportunities for rightsizing.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia wants to be a one-stop enterprise technology shop
item.description: After last week’s Nvidia GTC 2025 event, a new, fuller picture of the vendor is emerging. Analysts agree that Nvidia is not just a graphics chip provider anymore. It’s a full-stack solution provider, and GPUs are just one of many parts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
